title: Memory Audit Checklist
concept: Checking memory: episodic indexed, reflections scheduled, decay active, thread isolation ensured.

exercises.prompt: How did the agent fix a tool error in the last lesson?

options.text: By recalling a past episode where a similar error was fixed.
By asking for help from another agent.

exercises.explain: The agent successfully fixed the error by retrieving a relevant memory episode.

cards.title: Memory Audit Checklist
cards.body: A memory audit helps ensure that an agent's memory is working correctly to support task recovery.

cards.title: What is a Memory Audit?
cards.body: A memory audit is a check-up on an agent's memory, ensuring that important memories are stored properly and accessible.

cards.title: Like Organizing a Filing Cabinet
cards.body: Just as you need to keep a filing cabinet organized to find your documents easily, a memory audit ensures the agent can quickly retrieve the right memories when needed.

cards.title: Examples of Memory Checks
cards.body: Examples include checking if episodic memories are indexed correctly or verifying if decayed memories should be removed.

cards.title: Myth: Memory is Unchangeable
cards.body: Some think memories can't be adjusted. In reality, agents can update or remove memories during audits.

cards.title: Checklist for Memory Audit
cards.body: Use this checklist to verify the agent's memory status:

exercises.prompt: What should you check during a memory audit?

options.text: If episodic memories are indexed and decay is managed.
Whether the agent has a cool design.

exercises.explain: During a memory audit, you focus on checking if memories are properly indexed and if decay is managed.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A memory audit helps prevent important memories from being forgotten.
exercises.explain: True! Memory audits help maintain important memories and prevent them from being forgotten.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: A good memory audit should check if episodic memories are properly __________.
exercises.sentence: A good memory audit should check if episodic memories are properly indexed.
exercises.explain: Indexed is the term that describes how episodic memories should be organized for retrieval.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following actions is NOT part of a memory audit?

options.text: Checking if memories are organized properly.
Verifying if decayed memories are archived.

exercises.explain: Adding new memories without review bypasses the audit process and can lead to disorganization.

exercises.prompt: What is a main goal of performing a memory audit?

options.text: Fixing physical errors in the agent.
Ensuring the agent can recall important memories efficiently.
Updating the agent's software is the priority.

exercises.explain: The main goal of a memory audit is to ensure that the agent can efficiently recall important memories, which supports its ability to recover from errors. Remember, maintaining a robust memory aids in the agent's overall functionality.
